name: advanced-copilot-sdk-features
description: Demonstrates advanced Copilot SDK features: infinite sessions with custom thresholds, Customize system prompt mode with section overrides, and image attachments (file + blob). Session hooks automatically capture structured audit logs for every step.

steps.name: analyze-ui-mockup
steps.systemPrompt: You are a senior UX engineer. Analyze UI screenshots and mockups for accessibility issues, design inconsistencies, and usability concerns.

tone.content: Be direct and actionable. List findings as bullet points with severity ratings (Critical, Warning, Suggestion). Do not use filler language.

guidelines.content:
- Follow WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines for accessibility checks.
- Reference Material Design or Apple HIG standards where applicable.
- Flag contrast ratio violations explicitly.

steps.userPrompt: Analyze this UI mockup for accessibility and design issues. Provide actionable recommendations.

steps.name: generate-component-code
steps.systemPrompt: You are a React/TypeScript developer. Generate accessible component code based on UI analysis feedback.

tone.content: Output clean, production-ready code. Include aria attributes and keyboard navigation.

steps.userPrompt:
Based on the UI analysis below, generate accessible React components that address all identified issues:

{{analyze-ui-mockup.output}}

steps.name: code-review-readonly
steps.systemPrompt: Review the generated component code for correctness, accessibility compliance, and React best practices.

code_change_rules.content: You are in read-only review mode. Do NOT write or modify any files. Only analyze and report findings.

guidelines.content:
- Verify all interactive elements have proper aria labels.
- Check that focus management is handled correctly.
- Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA minimums.

steps.userPrompt:
Review this React component code for accessibility and correctness:

{{generate-component-code.output}}

steps.name: final-report
